TestCase z29Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    if (t1.n > 0) {
        int* follow_case_a = (int*)malloc(t1.n * sizeof(int));
        if (follow_case_a != NULL) {
            memcpy(follow_case_a, t1.a, t1.n * sizeof(int));
            follow_case.a = follow_case_a;
        } else {
            // malloc failed; return original follow_case without change
            return follow_case;
        }
    } else {
        // no elements to copy, just assign NULL pointer to follow_case.a to be safe
        follow_case.a = NULL;
    }
    // ------------------------------------

    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n < 2
    if (follow_case.n < 2) {
        // Less than 2 elements, no need to shuffle, return replica directly
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    // Fisher-Yates shuffle algorithm, using rand()
    for (int i = follow_case.n - 1; i > 0; i--) {
        // Defensive: ensure rand() range is valid, but rand() % (i+1) is safe since i >=1
        int j = rand() % (i + 1);
        // Swap follow_case.a[i] and follow_case.a[j]
        int temp = follow_case.a[i];
        follow_case.a[i] = follow_case.a[j];
        follow_case.a[j] = temp;
    }

    return follow_case;
}
